title: Boston’s innovation economy hits a new milestone in global rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Five Boston-area companies made the LexisNexis 'Top 100 Global Innovators' list, a record for the region.
LexisNexis praised Boston's innovation ecosystem, highlighting strengths in life sciences, consumer tech, and venture capital.
Orna Therapeutics was recently acquired by Eli Lilly for $2.4 billion, enhancing its innovation profile.

title: Is Scituate native Conor Garland a worthwhile trade target for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are considering a trade for Vancouver forward Conor Garland, who is signed through 2031-32 with a $6 million cap hit.
Bruins, holding a second wild-card playoff spot, might need additional middle-six scoring to enhance their postseason chances.
Potential trade for Garland could involve players like Matt Poitras and Mason Lohrei, impacting future team composition.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Acton home on 7 Concetta Circle sold for $1,175,000; built in 1988 with 2,726 sq ft, 4 bedrooms.
Boston condo at 100 Belvidere St. #8H, a high-rise built in 2001, sold for $3,700,000 with 2 bedrooms.
Arlington's 203 Everett St., a one-family ranch built in 1956, sold for $1,975,000 on a 6,965 sq ft lot.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots could target Colts wideout Alec Pierce in free agency after Colts use transition tag on QB Daniel Jones.
Alec Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, and could command $25-30 million annually.
Patriots may need to restructure Stefon Diggs' contract to afford Pierce, who could bolster New England's receiver corps.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, 34, was cited for petty larceny and unlawful mischief for allegedly discarding mail in North Troy.
Police found opened and unopened packages and holiday cards in the dumpster and her mail vehicle.
Morisseau is suspended from USPS and will appear in Vermont Superior Court on March 17 for the charges.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man in his 80s and a dog died in a Foxborough house fire early Tuesday morning.
The fire occurred on Granite Street and was reported just before 3 a.m. by a police officer.
The cause of the fire is under investigation; the home sustained significant damage despite quick suppression efforts.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's restaurant in Boston may face a license violation after hosting a controversial party with Patriots players.
Owner Helder George Brandao admitted responsibility, claiming players brought in hookahs, liquor, and strippers after an AFC win.
Police found 30 people, loud music, and evidence of smoking at the restaurant past the legal closing time.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the nation's highest scores on AP exams, with 35.8% scoring 3 or higher.
The state also saw the highest percentage of Black or African American students taking AP exams, outside Washington D.C.
Gov. Maura Healey aims to expand Early College programs and has subsidized over 90,783 AP exams for low-income students.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy city councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Five new Quincy city councilors are collecting a salary raise from $29,128.65 to $44,500, despite campaigning to repeal it.
New councilors plan to donate their raise portions to nonprofit organizations due to inability to repeal the raise immediately.
Quincy Mayor's proposed salary increase, initially $159,000 to $285,000, has been adjusted to $225,000 and delayed to 2028.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox are seen as a prime candidate to trade for Astros' Isaac Paredes, involving Jarren Duran.
Acquiring Paredes would address Red Sox's infield power needs, boosting their home run potential at Fenway Park.
Trading Duran could reduce Boston's outfield surplus, creating DH opportunities for Masataka Yoshida and Triston Casas.
